api_key.description: Cloudinary API key. Falls back to the CLOUDINARY_API_KEY environment variable.

api_secret.description: Cloudinary API secret. Falls back to the CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET environment variable.

cloud_name.description: Cloudinary cloud name. Falls back to the CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME environment variable.

cloudinary_url.description: Cloudinary URL in the format cloudinary://API_KEY:API_SECRET@CLOUD_NAME. Falls back to the CLOUDINARY_URL environment variable.

block.description: The Cloudinary provider manages product-environment-level Cloudinary resources such as folders.

external_id.description: The folder's external ID assigned by Cloudinary. Use this value in Cedar policy statements (e.g. resource.ancestor_ids.contains("...")).

id.description: The folder's external ID assigned by Cloudinary (same as external_id).

name.description: The leaf name of the folder (the last component of the path).

path.description: The full path of the folder (e.g. "production/images"). Segments are separated by "/". Must not start or end with "/". Forbidden characters: ? & # \ % < >

block.description: Manages a Cloudinary folder. Folders are used to organize assets in your Cloudinary account.

additive.description: When true, this global trigger fires in addition to any per-asset notification_url. Default false.

auth_scheme.description: Signature method for verifying incoming webhook payloads. One of: default, legacy_hmac, eddsa_v2.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the trigger was created.

event_type.description: The event that fires the trigger. One of: upload, delete, rename, move, eager, explode, multi, resource_tags_changed, resource_context_changed, resource_metadata_changed, resource_display_name_changed, access_control_changed, related_assets, create_folder, delete_folder, move_or_rename_asset_folder, proof_status_changed, error, all.

filter.description: A JSONLogic expression (JSON-encoded) that filters which events fire this trigger. When set, filter_language is automatically sent as "jsonlogic". Example: "{\"==\":[{\"var\":\"resource_type\"},\"image\"]}"

id.description: The unique identifier of the trigger assigned by Cloudinary.

payload_template.description: A Mustache template object (JSON-encoded, max 16KB) that customises the notification payload. Available namespaces: event, asset, folder, eager, error, raw.

product_environment_id.description: The product environment this trigger is scoped to.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the trigger was last updated.

uri.description: The webhook URL to which Cloudinary sends the notification payload.

uri_type.description: The type of the URI. Always "webhook".

block.description: Manages a Cloudinary webhook notification trigger. Triggers fire on specific asset events and POST a payload to a configured URL. Up to 30 triggers per product environment (the console UI shows only 10).

cloud_name.description: The Cloudinary cloud name the provider is configured for.

principal_id.description: The unique identifier of the current principal.

principal_type.description: The type of the current principal. Currently always "apiKey" when authenticating with a product environment API key.

block.description: Returns the identity of the currently authenticated principal (the credentials used to configure the provider). Use principal_id and principal_type directly in role assignments.

external_id.description: The folder's external ID assigned by Cloudinary. Use this value in Cedar policy statements (e.g. resource.ancestor_ids.contains("...")).

id.description: The folder's external ID assigned by Cloudinary (same as external_id).

name.description: The leaf name of the folder (the last component of the path).

path.description: The full path of the folder to look up (e.g. "production/images").

block.description: Reads information about an existing Cloudinary folder.

additive.description: Whether the trigger fires alongside per-asset notification_url callbacks.

auth_scheme.description: Signature method for verifying webhook payloads.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the trigger was created.

event_type.description: The event that fires the trigger. Used as a lookup key together with uri when id is not set.

id.description: The unique identifier of the trigger. When set, takes precedence over event_type + uri.

product_environment_id.description: The product environment this trigger is scoped to.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the trigger was last updated.

uri.description: The webhook URL. Used as a lookup key together with event_type when id is not set.

uri_type.description: The type of the URI. Always "webhook".

block.description: Reads information about an existing Cloudinary webhook notification trigger. Look up by id, or by the combination of event_type and uri.
Returns all webhook notification triggers configured for the product environment.
